title: pi workflow state command contract
description: Agent-facing contract for `pi workflow state`. The skill, action, session id, handoff target, json mode, and force mode are CLI arguments; only write/clear accept a JSON object through --input or --input-file.

phase.description: Write/clear input alias normalized by the CLI to current_phase. Prefer current_phase when writing canonical state.

sessionId.description: Current Pi session id. For `pi workflow state`, pass this as `--session <id>` or PI_SESSION_ID, not inside --input.

workflowPhase.description: Known workflow phases across all built-in skills. The command validates skill-specific phases and allowed transitions using the workflow manifest.

statePatch.description: Partial state envelope patch for write/clear. Additional skill-owned fields are allowed and preserved; skill mismatch, unknown phases, and disallowed transitions fail closed.

readInput.description: No --input payload. Use `pi workflow state <skill> read --session <id> --json`.

activeInput.description: No --input payload and no skill positional argument. Use `pi workflow state active --session <id> --json`.

oneOf.description: Compatibility wrapper accepted by the CLI; the nested state object is unwrapped before validation.

writeInput.description: Payload for `--input` or `--input-file` with `pi workflow state <skill> write --session <id>`. Do not include sessionId in this object.

oneOf.description: Compatibility wrapper accepted by the CLI; the nested state object is unwrapped before validation.

clearInput.description: Optional payload for `pi workflow state <skill> clear --session <id>`. Usually omit --input; the manifest chooses the terminal clear phase.

handoffInput.description: No --input payload. Pass the target skill as `--to <skill>`.

doctorInput.description: No --input payload. Use `pi workflow state <skill> doctor --session <id> --json`.

x-pi-cli.session: Required for every state action. Pass `--session <id>` or set PI_SESSION_ID; unlike skill action commands, state does not read sessionId from --input.
x-pi-cli.force: Pass --force only for explicit recovery/repair; it bypasses normal terminal/corrupt-state protections and records forced receipt metadata.

read.description: Read the session-scoped state envelope for one workflow skill.

write.description: Merge a state patch through manifest phase and transition validation, write the skill state file, and sync active-state HUD metadata.

clear.description: Mark the skill inactive at the manifest clear target. Usually omit --input; use --force only for recovery.

handoff.description: Mark the source skill inactive with handoff_to and the target skill active with handoff_from. The target is a CLI option, not JSON input.

active.description: Read the active workflow HUD projection for the session. This action has no skill positional argument.

doctor.description: Check whether the state file is readable; corrupt state returns a recovery hint.

examples.description: Read ralplan state for the current session.
examples.command: pi workflow state ralplan read --session h-... --json

examples.description: Write a known ralplan phase; phase is accepted as an alias for current_phase.
examples.command: pi workflow state ralplan write --session h-... --input '{"phase":"planner","run_id":"ralplan-..."}' --json

examples.description: Write using the compatibility state wrapper.
examples.command: pi workflow state team write --session h-... --input '{"state":{"current_phase":"running","active":true}}' --json

examples.description: Handoff from deep-interview to ralplan; target is --to, not JSON input.
examples.command: pi workflow state deep-interview handoff --to ralplan --session h-... --json

examples.description: Read active HUD state; no skill argument and no --input.
